preview_text:
The model favors Uzbekistan at 46%, with DR Congo positioned as underdog at 24%. This 22-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial edge for Uzbekistan in the statistical forecast. DR Congo's path to victory is narrow but genuine; the model sees the underdog winning roughly one match in four.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 30%, the modal result. This relatively high draw probability indicates competitive balance in expected match flow, even as Uzbekistan carries the edge. The win probabilities are distributed unevenly—Uzbekistan's advantage is clear, yet the presence of a sizeable draw likelihood suggests neither side is projected to dominate decisively.

A stark divergence separates the model from both sportsbook and prediction market consensus. The market elevates DR Congo to 45%, nearly doubling the model's 24% figure, while compressing Uzbekistan to 29%. The disagreement spans roughly 20 percentage points on DR Congo and 17 points on Uzbekistan. This dashboard presents both estimates without adjudicating which reflects true match probability.
